location.office_time_required: optional access to office space in Irvine, CA

level_signals: 5+ years of Technical Program Management experience in Fintech
Leads high-stakes vendor relationships and defines infrastructure strategy

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of Technical Program Management experience in Fintech, with a proven track record of running complex projects that balance technical constraints with customer needs.

Strong foundational knowledge of the mechanics of apple pay, google pay, credit/debit cards, prepaid cards, and the nuances of working with payment processors, point of sale systems, and banking institutions.
An understanding of APIs, SQL, and the mechanics of payment processing and account linking (e.g., Plaid).
Deep understanding of account connectivity (e.g., Plaid, manual linking, and automated verification) and how to optimize these for conversion and security.
Expertise in data analysis and the ability to articulate using data the tradeoff between enhancing security postures against adding friction to the customer experience.
A mindset that prioritizes both the Acorns customer and our internal operations team, with the ability to build a seamless payment platform that reflects our brand.
Lead high-stakes relationships and technical integrations with processors, bank SaaS providers, payment vendors, and merchants.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Develop the strategy for how Acorns will leverage technologies like Recurly, Apple Pay, Google Pay, debit card providers, and ACH to reduce friction for our customers
Expand Acorns ecosystem beyond Apple Pay and Google Pay to include future integrations like Venmo, Zelle, and other emerging rails
A belief that your work is tied to your life's mission.

scope.surface: Payments, Billing, and Account Linking infrastructure for millions of customers
scope.business_model: B2C fintech / financial wellness app; subscription and micro-investing platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Account linking success and verification rates
core_responsibilities: Collaborate with Engineering, Finance, and Data Science to define and execute the payments and billing roadmap
Drive delivery of improvements for bank account connectivity and verification experiences
Develop strategy for leveraging Recurly, Apple Pay, Google Pay, debit card providers, and ACH
Expand Acorns ecosystem to include Venmo, Zelle, and other emerging payment rails
Drive workflow strategies and tools to scale internal team's efficiency in managing payment exceptions and billing inquiries
Lead high-stakes relationships and technical integrations with processors, bank SaaS providers, payment vendors, and merchants

compensation.equity_form: stock options and equity grants mentioned

green_flags: Remote-first organization with optional office access — no mandatory in-office requirement
Explicitly states 'You are not expected to have experience with all listed requirements'
Clear product surface and ownership area (Payments, Billing, Account Linking)
Equity (stock options) mentioned in addition to base
Well-defined responsibilities across infrastructure, vendor management, and customer experience
red_flags: E-Verify participation signals no visa sponsorship, though work authorization policy is not explicitly stated as 'no sponsorship' in the JD body
Broad scope (payments infrastructure, billing, account linking, vendor management, ops) may indicate a lean team or high expectations for a single TPM
Global scaling mentioned ('scaling our systems for global use') but company appears US-focused — scope ambiguity
No explicit mention of team size or reporting structure

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years requirement and comp range)
location.work_auth set to 'no-sponsorship' inferred from E-Verify participation; not explicitly stated as 'no sponsorship'
location.visa_sponsorship set to 'no' inferred from E-Verify participation
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'base pay offered may vary depending on multiple individualized factors, including market location')
ambiguities: Work authorization: JD mentions E-Verify participation but does not explicitly say 'no sponsorship.' Treated as no-sponsorship based on E-Verify being a common signal of that policy.
Several responsibilities (e.g., Recurly, Venmo, Zelle integrations) are framed as forward-looking goals rather than required past experience — classified as nice-to-haves on that basis.
JD closing line ('Would you like me to refine the technical requirements further...') appears to be an AI-generated artifact accidentally included in the JD body — not content from Acorns.
